author: Kaelen
content:
TASK: Making info@ able to send emails through Dev Dashboard

I dug into this on May 10 so we could separate what is actually blocked from what is already working.

info@ mailbox access is confirmed, and the Resend invite has been accepted. Supabase already has a RESEND_API_KEY secret from May 8, so I would not rotate or replace that key unless later testing proves it is bad.

The Dev Dashboard send path was tested. I found one app-side issue: the send-email calls were not including Supabase auth headers. I have a local code fix for that now. With those headers in place, the function reaches Resend.

The current blocker is DNS/domain verification. Resend is rejecting sends because thenorthstarhouse.org is not verified. SPF/MX records are verified, but the IONOS TXT value for resend._domainkey does not match the DKIM value shown in Resend.

Access is the hard stop. IONOS login routes through admin@thenorthstarhouse.org. That mailbox is currently blocked by Google 2FA to a phone we do not control. Next step is to recover admin mailbox/2FA access, or have the DNS/account owner replace the existing resend._domainkey TXT value, restart Resend verification, and retest sending.

---
TASK: Define reply monitoring plan for membership emails

I confirmed info@ mailbox access. If info@ is the reply inbox we want to use, we can define reply-monitoring ownership, cadence, and escalation separately from the IONOS/DKIM work.

The current DNS blocker affects authenticated sending through Resend. It does not block basic inbox access.
